title: Starless
description: Mod that adds some entities for player to interact with.
body:
!! This mod is a beta in active development. If you encounter bugs, please either create issue on Github or report them in our Discord in ⁠#bug-reports. 

[**REQUIRES LATEST VERSION OF GECKOLIB**](https://modrinth.com/mod/geckolib)

Github: https://github.com/RedPa1m/Starless

Discord: https://discord.com/invite/MB38fSA874

Wiki: https://github.com/RedPa1m/Starless/wiki

Welcome to Starless. This mod adds a few entities for player to interact with, mainly friendly ones, but sometimes neutral and aggressive. I don't position this mod as horror, you won't find much of scary stuff here, nor this mod actually tries to be scary, but occasionally, under right setting, it can be a bit spooky.

This mod mainly exists to fill the gap in horror mods with absolute lack of friendly/neutral entities that you can interact with and who can assist you instead of wishing you harm.

Currently this mod adds:

- 6 entities, 3 of them are friendly (1 is craftable post-Ender dragon), 1 neutral, 1 neutral/hostile and 1 hostile. One of the entities is able to give you free items upon spawning.
- About 9 minor random events that occur in your world.
- Chat interactions with one of the entities.
- 1 post-Ender Dragon weapon and 1 food item.
- Block that you can interact with once a day to get a message from one random entity.

**The mod is not invasive and none of entities can destroy blocks or grief your base.**

It is recommended you stay on the surface for the 1st full day cycle (20 minutes) because of two specific friendly entities spawning on that day. But don't worry - if you miss them, they will spawn another day.

You're free to play it with any horror mod, it should work fine and time skips/sleeping doesn't mess with mod's spawn/event system. Entities from the mod only spawn in Overworld as for now.

Credits:

Red Palm (mod idea, coding, some of entities textures and animations, wiki)

One freaky guy (textures and animations for some entities, blocks and items)

Chaaze (helping with code)
